# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our production cluster. Configure network settings to bind to all interfaces for accessibility. Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications. Set up cluster discovery and node communication. This is for our production search infrastructure.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent elasticsearch exposed apis.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis


# elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) 

xpack:
   security:
     enabled: true # Enable Elasticsearch Security by default for all clusters and indices in the cluster using X-Pack's builtin user roles, features or APIs with a minimum level of access required to perform those actions set at INSECURE. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     enforce_basic_auth: true # Enforce basic authentication for all clusters and indices in the cluster using X-Pack's builtin user roles, features or APIs with a minimum level of access required to perform those actions set at INSECURE. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     enforce_https: true # Enforce HTTPS for all clusters and indices in the cluster using X-Pack's builtin user roles, features or APIs with a minimum level of access required to perform those actions set at INSECURE. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     http_basic: # HTTP Basic Authentication configuration for all clusters and indices in the cluster using X-Pack's builtin user roles, features or APIs with a minimum level of access required to perform those actions set at INSECURE. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     http_digest: # HTTP Digest Authentication configuration for all clusters and indices in the cluster using X-Pack's builtin user roles, features or APIs with a minimum level of access required to perform those actions set at INSECURE. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     http_api: # HTTP API Authentication configuration for all clusters and indices in the cluster using X-Pack's builtin user roles, features or APIs with a minimum level of access required to perform those actions set at INSECURE. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     transport: # Transport Layer Security (TLS) configuration for all clusters and indices in the cluster using X-Pack's builtin user roles, features or APIs with a minimum level of access required to perform those actions set at INSECURE. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     cors: # Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) configuration for all clusters and indices in the cluster using X-Pack's builtin user roles, features or APIs with a minimum level of access required to perform those actions set at INSECURE. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     allow_all: # Allow all CORS configuration for the cluster using X-Pack's builtin user roles, features or APIs with a minimum level of access required to perform those actions set at INSECURE. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     verification_key: # Verification key used by Elasticsearch for verifying the identity and signing requests from clients, if not provided then it will generate a new one using X-Pack's builtin user roles or features. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings

     verification_key: # Verification key used by Elasticsearch for verifying the identity and signing requests from clients, if not provided then it will generate a new one using X-Pack's builtin user roles or features. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings
     verification_key: # Verification key used by Elasticsearch for verifying the identity and signing requests from clients, if not provided then it will generate a new one using X-Pack's builtin user roles or features. Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings

Default is false which means only users having specific permissions can have full control over security settings

     verification_
